news.headline: A decade-old event, the State of Latino Entrepreneurship Summit on March 27, 2025, anticipates 1,200 attendees focusing on post-pandemic trends and challenges.
news.summary: In Palo Alto, California, the Latino Business Action Network (LBAN) and Stanford Graduate School of Business are joining forces for the 10th consecutive year to present the latest insights on Latino Entrepreneurship in the U.S. The 2025 State of Latino Entrepreneurship (SOLE) Summit, scheduled for March 27, 2025, will delve into the challenges facing Latino-owned businesses, their significant impact on the economy, and emerging trends post-pandemic.This event, which is free to attend, aims to not only share research findings but also facilitate networking, ecosystem development, and action-oriented dialogues. With over 1,200 participants expected, including entrepreneurs, investors, partners, policymakers, and industry experts, the summit seeks to drive tangible changes and forge a supportive community to drive the future of Latino entrepreneurship and economic development in the country.

news.headline: No new comments from Trump's pre-recorded address at the Digital Asset Summit.
Five Below Inc. (NASDAQ:FIVE) surpasses Q4 earnings expectations, with shares climbing. Analysts cautious due to tariff challenges.
news.summary: Five Below Inc. (NASDAQ:FIVE) saw an increase in its stock price during early trading on Thursday following the announcement of positive fourth-quarter earnings. Analyst Matthew Boss from JPMorgan maintained an Underweight rating and set a price target of $98 for the stock. Five Below exceeded expectations by reporting earnings of $3.48 per share, surpassing the anticipated $3.37 per share. Additionally, the company's same-store sales of -3.0% outperformed the predicted -3.4%. Boss noted an improvement in comparable sales in the latter part of the year. Management also provided guidance for adjusted earnings between $4.10 and $4.72 per share for 2025. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Commercial Metals' Q2 2025 net sales dropped 5.1% YoY to $1.754B, with Core EBITDA at $130.995M, down from $212.09M, and adjusted EPS of 26 cents (down from 73 cents). Finished steel shipments increased by 3.3% YoY, maintaining confidence in construction sector with $758.4M in cash and $1.6B in available liquidity. Repurchased 906,603 shares for $48M.
news.summary: In the second quarter of 2025, Commercial Metals Company (NYSE:CMC) announced financial results showing a 5.1% decrease in net sales to $1.754 billion compared to the previous year. This figure was close to the analyst forecast of $1.746 billion. Core EBITDA dropped to $130.995 million from $212.09 million in the same quarter of the prior year, resulting in a core EBITDA margin decline from 11.5% to 7.5%. Adjusted EPS stood at 26 cents, a decrease from 73 cents in the previous year and below the expected 30 cents. The company experienced a 3.3% increase in finished steel shipments due to strong demand in North American construction. Their backlog grew substantially driven by new project awards. As of February 28, 2025, Commercial Metals had $758.4 million in cash and nearly $1.6 billion in available liquidity. During the quarter, they repurchased 906,603 shares for $48 million, leaving $305.3 million available under the buyback plan. On March 19, 2025, Commercial Metals declared a quarterly dividend.

news.headline: Radware listed as a Strong Performer in Forrester report on Web Application Firewall Solutions, Q1 2025. Top 10 WAF vendors.
news.summary: In Mahwah, New Jersey on March 20, 2025, Radware®, a renowned company specializing in application security and delivery solutions for multi-cloud environments, has been recognized as a Strong Performer in The Forrester Wave: Web Application Firewall Solutions, Q1 2025. The company, listed on NASDAQ as RDWR, was one of the top 10 web application firewall (WAF) vendors evaluated in the latest market report.

news.headline: Tax agencies will intensify crypto monitoring before Bitcoin hits $1M. US IRS mandates wallet cost tracking by 2025. Global data sharing increasing. Misinformed investors at risk. Watch out for audits. Crypto gains under scrutiny. Cooperation between tax authorities rising. Updated guidance for decentralized exchanges delayed. Tax experts joining authorities.
news.summary: Robin Singh, CEO of Koinly, warns that tax agencies worldwide are ramping up their efforts to track and tax crypto investments as Bitcoin approaches all-time highs. Governments are improving their automated data-sharing systems to catch up with crypto activity on centralized exchanges. In the US, the IRS now mandates using the wallet-by-wallet cost tracking method, increasing data collection. Countries like Australia use alerts to monitor crypto activity and may increase audit risk for non-reporting taxpayers. Global tax cooperation is strengthening, with agreements to exchange tax information between countries. Tax authorities are also focusing on decentralized finance and non-fungible tokens, even though tracking is more challenging. Overall, investors need to be prepared for increased scrutiny and potential audits as tax agencies become more sophisticated in monitoring and taxing crypto investments.

news.headline: L2 solutions in crypto may be a hindrance rather than a fix for scalability issues, risking decentralization and security. Focusing on L1 development for better results. #Crypto #ScalabilityIssues #L1Development
news.summary: In a critical opinion piece by Dan Hughes, the founder of Radix, he questions the reliance on layer-2 (L2) solutions in the cryptocurrency space, highlighting how these solutions meant to address scalability issues may actually be undermining decentralization and security. The proliferation of L2s has led to a fragmented ecosystem with complex rollups and bridging solutions, centralizing parts of the network and introducing security risks. Hughes argues that rather than focusing on band-aid solutions, the industry should prioritize building scalable and secure foundations at layer 1, with a potential solution being state sharding to enhance decentralization and accessibility without compromising security. By emphasizing the importance of L1 development over L2 patches, Hughes advocates for a future where blockchain networks prioritize decentralization, efficiency, and security to achieve widespread adoption and avoid potential pitfalls in the industry.

news.headline: US to sell $68 bln 6-month bills on March 24th, to settle on March 27th.
US to sell $69 bln 2-year notes on March 25th, to settle on March 31st.
US to sell $70 bln 5-year notes on March 26th, to settle on March 31st.
US to sell $44 bln 7-year notes on March 27th, to settle on March 31st.

XDAO protocol confers legal status to 367k DAOs through TON-based tech. Telegram bot allows binding docs signing.ereum wallets and blockchain tech utilized for DES compliant contracts, Telegram bot documents. Arbitration via messenger, Docusign, Ethsign for dispute resolution.
news.summary: XDAO, a protocol based on The Open Network (TON), has successfully facilitated legal status for more than 367,000 decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) through automating the recognition process. They have streamlined the creation of DAOs to obtain legal recognition by establishing a standard for sub-entities within their legal framework. XDAO operates from Singapore as its main jurisdiction for dispute resolution. The protocol enables signing of legally binding documents using Web3 wallets and archiving transactions using a Telegram bot. The legality of agreements formed through the bot is recognized in most jurisdictions, although it may have limitations in areas like real estate and securities contracts. XDAO also offers smart contract-based compliance models for arbitration scenarios, where arbitration agreements can be formed via messenger or e-signature methods. In case of payment disputes, an arbitrator can be added to the DAO with the right to a key vote for consensus.

news.headline: ClostraBio secures investment from INVENT Illinois and NextGen Nutrition, plans product launch in 2025.
news.summary: A biotechnology company in Chicago, ClostraBio, Inc., is emphasizing natural pathways to support gut health. They have disclosed additions of institutional investors in their recent bridge financing round, which now includes backing from the Illinois Innovation Venture Fund (INVENT) and NextGen Nutrition Investment Partners (NGN). The INVENT initiative, managed by the Office of Entrepreneurship, Innovation & Technology at Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity (DCEO), is part of this development.

news.headline: Forza Capital renews $40 million credit facility for Sell Wholesale Funding (SWF) to expand services and reach a wider audience.
news.summary: Phoenix and Tampa, Florida - Forza Capital and SWF have enjoyed a prosperous partnership for almost twenty years. The recent renewal of their credit facility showcases Forza's trust in SWF's capacity to deliver excellent financial solutions to clients. The financing will empower SWF to enhance its offerings and extend its outreach, offering highly competitive products in the market. The collaboration with Sabin Burrell has been significant, driving the success of both parties in the industry.

Dream Office REIT to distribute 8.333 cents per unit in March 2025 ($1.00 annualized), payable on April 15, 2025.
news.summary: Dream Office REIT (TSX: D.UN) has disclosed a monthly distribution of 8.333 cents ($1.00 annualized) per REIT Unit, Series A ("REIT A Units") for March 2025. The distribution will be paid on April 15, 2025, to unitholders as of March 31, 2025. Dream Office REIT, an open-ended real estate investment trust, is a prominent office landlord in downtown Toronto, possessing more than 3.5 million square feet of office space.

news.headline: Dream Industrial REIT declares March 2025 distribution of 5.833 cents per Unit (70 cents annualized), payable on April 15.
news.summary: Dream Industrial REIT (TSX: DIR.UN) disclosed its March 2025 monthly distribution of 5.833 cents per Unit (70 cents annually). The payment is scheduled for April 15, 2025, for unitholders on record as of March 31, 2025. The REIT, managing various industrial properties worldwide, reported as of December 31, 2024.

news.headline: Dream Residential REIT announces March 2025 monthly distribution of US$0.035 per unit (US$0.42 annualized), payable on April 15.
news.summary: In Toronto, Dream Residential Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX: DRR.U and TSX: DRR.UN) declared its March 2025 monthly distribution at US$0.035 per unit (US$0.42 annually). The distribution is scheduled to be paid on April 15, 2025, to unitholders recorded as of March 31, 2025. Dream Residential REIT is an open-ended real estate investment trust.

news.headline: BoE Gov. Bailey: There's no sign yet of job cuts in early indicators.

February saw a 4.2% monthly increase in US existing home sales with a median price nearing $400k. Analysts predict a potential surge in the market due to rising prices and tight inventory. Inventory is at 1.24 million units representing 3.5 months' worth of supply, still below the balanced threshold.
news.summary: In February, existing home sales saw a significant 4.2% monthly increase, reaching a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.26 million homes. Despite being down 1.2% from the previous year, this marked a notable improvement from months of stagnation. Median home prices also rose by 3.8% year-over-year, nearing the $400,000 mark, marking the 20th consecutive month of price gains. The housing market continues to grapple with tight inventory, with the supply of unsold homes increasing by 5.1% to 1.24 million units. However, this only represents 3.5 months' worth of supply, far below the balanced threshold of six months for buyers and sellers. Analysts see this as potentially setting the stage for further price increases and a spike in sales, despite concerns about affordability.

news.headline: Nike's Q3 earnings dropping to 29 cents/share from 77 cents last year; revenue expected at $11.01B vs. $12.43B. Stocks closed at $72.99, down 0.4%.
news.summary: Sportswear giant NIKE, Inc. (NYSE:NKE) is set to announce its third-quarter financial results on March 20. Analysts predict earnings to be around 29 cents per share, a significant drop from the 77 cents per share reported in the same period last year. The company is expected to post quarterly revenue of $11.01 billion, a decrease from $12.43 billion a year ago. NIKE has surpassed revenue expectations in the second quarter and has beaten analyst revenue estimates in six out of the last ten quarters. On Wednesday, NIKE's shares closed at $72.99 after a 0.4% decrease. For additional details on analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.com.

"Keep America Beautiful's campaign challenges every American to pick up 25 pieces of litter by Earth Day 2025; survey shows Dwayne Johnson as top inspiration."
news.summary: In a recent survey conducted among Americans, it was found that Dwayne “The Rock” Johnson is the celebrity who most motivates them to engage in cleaning up litter. The survey gathered responses from a substantial number of individuals across the country, highlighting the significant impact Johnson has on inspiring positive actions. This result indicates the strong influence of celebrities in promoting environmental consciousness, with Johnson leading the way in encouraging responsible waste disposal practices among the public.

news.headline: SEC under Trump drops lawsuits and investigations against crypto firms. Biggest targets now off the hook.
news.summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, under the leadership of President Trump, has shifted its stance towards crypto firms, choosing to de-escalate conflicts instead of pursuing aggressive action. This change in approach is evidenced by the increasing number of former targets of SEC scrutiny within the crypto industry. The regulatory agency has opted to step back from confrontations with these companies, reflecting a notable shift in priorities.

news.headline: French President Macron failed to secure private equity - statement by JPMorgan exec. #France #Macron #PrivateEquity
news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global economy is projected to grow by 4.5% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The report also highlighted that the recovery varies across regions, with advanced economies expected to grow by 5.6% while emerging markets and developing economies are projected to grow by 6.3%. Despite this positive outlook, concerns remain about disparities in vaccine distribution and the potential for new variants of the virus to impact progress. Additionally, global trade volume is forecasted to increase by 8.4% in 2021 after a 5.3% decline in the previous year. This growth is attributed to the easing of pandemic-induced restrictions and increased economic activity. The report emphasized the need for continued policy support to ensure a sustainable and inclusive recovery.

news.headline: Dow Jones reverses on surprise data, Apple, Nvidia, Tesla rise; key economic indicators impacted the market.
news.summary: Today, the Dow Jones experienced a turnaround following unexpected economic data, with Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla all showing gains on the stock market. This highlights the impact of the surprising economic information on the market. This news is significant for investors tracking the performance of these companies and the overall market trends. The reversal in the Dow Jones reflects the volatility and sensitivity of the market to key economic indicators. Investors should closely monitor these developments as they provide insights into the market's reactions and the performance of major companies like Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla.

news.headline: Sanofi acquires Dren Bio's DR-0201 for about $2B, targeting myeloid cells to induce B-cell depletion in autoimmune diseases.
news.summary: Sanofi SA (listed on NASDAQ as SNY) announced its acquisition of Dren Bio Inc.'s DR-0201, a targeted bispecific myeloid cell engager (MCE). DR-0201 is a CD20-directed bispecific antibody designed to engage specific myeloid cells for B-cell depletion through targeted phagocytosis. Early clinical study findings in autoimmune disorders propose that deep B-cell depletion could potentially reset the adaptive immune system, leading to sustained treatment-free remission in patients with refractory B-cell-mediated autoimmune conditions such as lupus. The news of FDA approval for Sanofi's Merilog as the first rapid-acting insulin biosimilar can be found in full detail on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Disclosure: Form 8.3 filed for Primary Health Properties Plc, with significant updates on their shareholder positions.
Zelestra secures 500MW hybrid project contract with SJVN in India for renewable energy supply.
news.summary: Zelestra, a renewable energy company based in Madrid, has recently secured a significant contract from Satluj Jal Vidyut Nigam (SJVN), a prominent Indian public sector company. The contract, valued as a Firm & Dispatchable Renewable Energy (FDRE) agreement, allows Zelestra to construct a groundbreaking hybrid megaproject with a capacity of 500 MW. This transformative project will integrate solar, wind, and energy storage technologies. Zelestra's focus on customized, diverse energy solutions played a crucial role in winning this contract.

news.headline: Coronis Health uses UiPath Platform for advanced AI to improve healthcare systems ensuring transparency and compliance.
news.summary: In SYKESVILLE, Maryland, Coronis Health, a prominent healthcare revenue cycle management provider, has utilized the UiPath Platform™ to improve its healthcare systems. This move is part of Coronis' strategy for digital transformation, enabling the integration of advanced AI features. By leveraging the UiPath Platform, Coronis is boosting transparency, security, compliance, and ethical governance across automated processes. This step is significant in enhancing operational efficiency and ensuring high standards in healthcare delivery systems.

news.headline: 1/3 women have no pressing gender concerns in Women's History Month as stress continues to rise; 34% with no issues.
news.summary: In Washington, March is recognized as Women’s History Month, focusing on advocating for gender equality and women's welfare. Surprisingly, a recent survey of more than 2,000 adults aged 21 and above, conducted by Northerner.com, discovered that 34% of women do not perceive any significant gender equality concerns in their lives. This data highlights a contrasting perspective as stress levels among women in the U.S. continue to escalate. The study, which took place in 2025, shows that only 9% highlighted gender equity as a priority issue. This indicates a critical need for continued efforts to address and raise awareness about gender equality issues affecting women.

news.headline: Cousins Maine Lobster debuts 23rd food truck in Albany, NY, expanding presence with fast-casual lobster rolls.
news.summary: Experienced operators have introduced their 23rd truck, increasing the presence of the Cousins Maine Lobster (CML) brand in New York. The industry-leading fast-casual lobster roll business has expanded its reach in New York by launching a new food truck in Albany on March 20, 2025.

news.headline: Russian Civic Chamber suggests creating fund for seized crypto assets to benefit state, help with social projects amid legal progresses. Evgeny Masharov proposes government cryptocurrency fund; aims to use confiscated assets for gov't revenues, projects.Source: Reuters.
news.summary: Legal activists in Russia, led by Evgeny Masharov, proposed establishing a government cryptocurrency fund using assets seized from criminal activities. This fund aims to generate revenue for the government to support various social, environmental, and educational projects. Masharov's proposal aligns with new legislation recognizing cryptocurrencies as property for criminal procedure purposes in Russia. Russian authorities have been seizing crypto assets for years, with no clear framework on how these funds should be distributed. The Bank of Russia governor, Elvira Nabiullina, has opposed investing in crypto due to its volatility, contrasting with initiatives like the US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. This move highlights the ongoing debate on the potential benefits and risks of integrating cryptocurrencies into government reserves.

news.headline: Americans' stress levels are rising with 47% turning to friends/family and 15% using substances to cope. Study surveyed over 2,000 U.S. adults, showing generational differences.
news.summary: In the United States, stress levels are increasing as the country faces a new political landscape and continued economic difficulties. A survey of more than 2,000 American adults aged 21 and older discovered that 47% seek support from friends and family, while 15% resort to substances like alcohol, cigarettes, traditional tobacco, and cannabis as coping mechanisms. The research, carried out by Censuswide on behalf of Nicokick.com, highlighted generational variances in stress levels.

news.headline: Waymo gets mapping permit for SFO, after previously failing in 2023. Permitted on March 14 for robotaxi services.
news.summary: Waymo, a subsidiary of Alphabet, Inc., has been granted a temporary permit to map roadways at San Francisco International Airport (SFO). The permit, effective since March 14, is a significant achievement for Waymo's robotaxi services expansion. San Francisco Mayor Daniel Lurie announced the permit, indicating a turnaround for Waymo after its previous unsuccessful attempt to secure mapping access to SFO in 2023. This development marks a milestone in Waymo's efforts to enhance its autonomous driving technology. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Designer Brands Inc (NYSE:DBI) stock rises despite sales decline of 5.4% in Q4 to $713.572M, but EPS loss beats estimate. Gross profit margin up 80 bps to 39.6% but gross profit declined 3.4% to $282.6M. Total comps sales rose by 0.5%.
news.summary: Shares of Designer Brands Inc (NYSE:DBI) surged following the company's fourth-quarter earnings report. The quarter saw a 5.4% decrease in sales, dropping to $713.572 million, below the anticipated $719.323 million. However, total comparable sales grew by 0.5%. Adjusted EPS loss was recorded at 44 cents, surpassing the predicted 49 cents loss. Gross profit margin increased by 80 basis points to 39.6%, while gross profit fell 3.4% to $282.6 million. The business reported an operating loss.

news.headline: Turkey raises the overnight lending rate to 46% at the interim meeting.
Viamericas launches cash-to-cash money transfer service in the U.S. at thousands of agent locations, making transfers easier.
news.summary: In Coral Gables, Florida, Viamericas has introduced a new domestic cash-to-cash money transfer service in the fintech sector. This service is now accessible at numerous agent locations throughout the United States.
